note: A 36-key (3x5 + 3 thumb keys) wireless ortholinear split keyboard designed specifically to support either standard Choc v1 switches or ultra-low profile Kailh X switches.
open-source (DIY)
open-source (DIY)

A split ergonomic keyboard featuring a 40mm Cirque trackpad that can be hot-swapped with an OLED display, integrated EC12 rotary encoders, MX hotswap sockets, and support for Helios or Liatris RP2040 microcontrollers via QMK firmware.

A 36-key wireless split keyboard featuring a PCB generated via Ergogen. It supports both Choc v1 and v2 switches using native hotswap sockets, features custom 3D-printed lever keycaps, and includes a Molex Pico Ezmate battery connector alongside a dedicated hardware power switch.
open-source (DIY)
A 34-key (5x3+2) minimal split keyboard heavily inspired by the Sweep, featuring an aggressive column stagger, aggressive thumb tuck, integrated power switch, and native Kailh Choc hotswap sockets.

An ergonomic, split, 40%, 3D key-welled, handwired keyboard utilizing WeAct RP2040 microcontrollers, TRRS split communication, and a Panasonic EVQWGD001 rotary encoder on the bottom-most thumb key of each half.

keyswitch: Kailh Choc / Gateron Low Profile / Cherry MX
note: A 36-key wireless-only split keyboard with a high-density 19mm x 16mm switch layout. Features integrated battery cutouts, a hardware power switch, and a print-in-place hinged magnetic travel case design in the repository.
A 36-key (2x3x5+3) column-staggered split keyboard. A port of the CH552-36 to the RISC-V CH32X MCU, keeping the footprint under 100x100mm.
A 48-key (4x12) un-split ortholinear board. Essentially a port of the CH552-48 to the more powerful RISC-V CH32X MCU with Rust firmware support.
A large 75-key (5x15) un-split ortholinear keyboard running on a RISC-V CH32X controller, designed specifically for cheap low-profile Redragon switches and tray-mounting in a standard 60% case.
An ultra low-budget 36-key (2x3x5+3) column-staggered split keyboard utilizing a non-reversible SMT component design with a CH552 MCU.
A very low-budget 44-key un-split ortholinear keyboard using the CH552 MCU and a specialized 7x7 matrix to save GPIO pins.
A 48-key (4x12) un-split ortholinear layout using the low-cost CH552 MCU, matching BM40/JJ40 dimensions.
A variation of the CH552-48 designed specifically around ultra-affordable Redragon low-profile switches.
The classic standard-spaced MX switch variant of the 42-key Corne split keyboard layout, optimized for ZMK wireless environments.
A specialized variant of the Corne Cherry designed with specific physical structural cutouts or structural mounting hole provisions to support integrated tilting and tenting plates.
The low-profile variant of the 42-key layout engineered explicitly for Kailh Choc v1 switches using tighter, more compact Choc spacing.
The 'Classic' or original Corne 42-key split keyboard design. This is the standard 6-column, 3-row + 3-thumb key layout that serves as the baseline for the entire crkbd ecosystem.

keyswitch: Kailh Cherry MX / Kailh Choc v1
note: Available in multiple variants including Corne Cherry (MX) and Corne Chocolate (Choc). The v4 variants feature an integrated MCU on board (RP2040) instead of a breakout board slot, support 42 keys by default (6x3 column/row grid + 3 thumb keys per side), and have break-off outer columns to reduce the layout to a 36-key configuration.

keyswitch: Kailh Cherry MX / Kailh Choc v1
note: SergioRibera's repository fork of the popular 42-key Corne split keyboard layout.
A stripped-back, budget version of the Corne layout utilizing a thinner PCB substrate and eliminating hotswap sockets in favor of basic through-hole components.
A custom split mechanical keyboard project focused on an ergonomic layout flow.
A 36-key (3x5+3) split columnar-staggered ergonomic keyboard configuration designed with custom ergonomics.

A 36-key (5x3+3) wireless low-profile split keyboard with an aggressive 35-degree angle pinky splay, aggressively staggered column clusters, and a 110-degree angled thumb cluster designed to comfortably fit small-to-medium hands without stretching.

A diodeless, 36-key wireless split keyboard featuring a reversible PCB designed specifically for Choc v1 switches. Powered by nice!nano microcontrollers with a Seeed XIAO BLE dongle, it features aggressive custom splay on all columns and integrated case magnets for travel.
open-source (DIY)
A 36-key choc-spaced unibody split ortholinear keyboard featuring an integrated SSD1306 OLED display and a Pro Micro controller footprint.

A 42-key (6x3+3) split ergonomic keyboard strongly inspired by the Corne, but updated to use standard 1N4148 through-hole diodes exclusively, an optional rotary encoder on the innermost thumb key, and an altered aggressive pinky column stagger.

Features a slanted unibody design inspired by Le Chifre and Le Oeuf. Uses ultra-low profile wafer-thin Kailh notebook switches that require surface mount hotplate/reflow soldering or specialized bottom-side PCB soldering via cutouts.
A diodeless 36-key split low-profile keyboard created using Ergogen and KiCad, running on dual RP2040-Zero microcontrollers with optional Kailh Choc hotswap sockets and inner thumb splay.
A 36-key low-profile travel split keyboard utilizing Seeed Studio XIAO RP2040 microcontrollers, a reversible PCB design, and support for hotswap or solder-mounted configurations.
A 42-key un-split ortholinear keyboard using a Raspberry Pi Pico devboard positioned so it doesn't sit underneath the PCB, matching BM40/JJ40 mounting holes.

A 36-key (3x5+3) wireless splayed split low-profile keyboard that utilizes Seeed Studio XIAO BLE microcontrollers for both halves and a dedicated wireless dongle. Features Kailh Choc hotswap sockets, integrated hardware power switches, and white LED backlighting.
open-source (DIY)
A 36-key columnar split keyboard designed for Gateron KS27 low-profile switches and a Blackpill controller. Features a diodeless matrix, tenting design, and trackpoint support.
A 36-key columnar split keyboard featuring a compact 9.2x9.2cm PCB layout. Uses a Blackpill controller, a diodeless matrix, supports trackpoint modules, and accommodates multiple types of switches.
A 36-key columnar split keyboard designed around standard Cherry MX switches with integrated hotswap sockets. Powered by a Raspberry Pi Pico using a diodeless layout with trackpoint support.

keyboard: pskeeb4 gateron low profile ks27 hotswap
note: A 36-key columnar split keyboard utilizing Gateron Low Profile KS27 switches with native hotswap sockets, powered by an RP2040-Zero controller and featuring trackpoint support.

keyboard: pskeeb5 bluetooth kailh choc v1/v2 hotswap
note: The fifth iteration of the PSKEEB line, featuring a 36-key wireless columnar split design. Built around a nice!nano v2 controller running ZMK, supporting trackpoint modules, rotary encoders, and a portable stackable case design.
A custom low-profile split mechanical keyboard utilizing Seeed Studio XIAO controllers and Kailh Choc switches.
Features hotswap sockets and per-key RGB lighting in a BM40/JJ40-compatible Planck-MIT layout.
A simple 44-key (Planck-MIT 4x12 layout with 1x2U option) un-split ortholinear PCBA that is BM40/JJ40-compatible.

A 36-key low-profile split keyboard derived from the GergoPlex, featuring 18x17mm Choc spacing, hybrid hotswap or direct solder PCB options, and OLED display support. Available as a kit or pre-built on Holykeebs.
A 34-key wireless low-profile split keyboard variant of the Ferris Sweep, optimized for ZMK firmware, featuring an integrated power switch and battery support.
A 36-key split keyboard featuring integrated Kailh hotswap sockets, a rotary encoder on each half, and support for 0.96-inch OLED displays.
A split ergonomic keyboard from the fingerpunch ecosystem that features Seeed XIAO controller support, tight column stagger, and optional integrated pointer/trackball modules.
A 60-key (5x12) un-split ortholinear keyboard adapting the Lumberjack 'show the components' style. Uses the budget RISC-V CH582 BLE board with integrated LiPo charging.
A 36-key (2x3x5+3) split, column-staggered keyboard supporting both MX and Choc switches. Features a reversible sub-100x100mm PCB, per-key RGB, optional OLED, and optional rotary encoder.
A 60-key (5x12) un-split ortholinear keyboard that is GH60-compatible. Designed in a 'show the components' style adapting the Lumberjack layout.
A variant of the X-2 un-split ortholinear keyboard adding Kailh MX Hotswap sockets and per-key/underglow RGB lighting.
